rejected_candidates.title: GatewaySend destination execution uses nominal `amount` without validating actual ERC20 intake
rejected_candidates.reason: Duplicate of existing F-005 root cause: destination `onCall` trusts payload amount/token and does not reconcile actual intake; soft-fail/under-receipt variants are already covered.

rejected_candidates.title: Native-input fee transfer silently no-ops when `_ETH_ADDRESS_` is used as token address
rejected_candidates.reason: Subsumed by F-004 sentinel-input underfunding/theft path; standalone fee-accounting symptom is not a distinct reportable root cause.

rejected_candidates.title: Refund-claimed event logs zero token/amount due storage-delete ordering
rejected_candidates.reason: Observability-only issue; does not create realistic protocol-level fund loss, theft, lockup, or DoS by itself.

rejected_candidates.title: Packed-message parser can read token addresses outside declared `crossChainData` length
rejected_candidates.reason: No additional exploit impact beyond already attacker-controlled payload/token fields in existing critical findings (notably F-005).

rejected_candidates.title: TransferNative revert handlers lack minimum `revertMessage` length checks
rejected_candidates.reason: Low-confidence/non-realistic trigger: callbacks are `onlyGateway` and this contract’s own revert-message construction always prefixes at least 32 bytes.

rejected_candidates.title: RefundInfo entry can be overwritten causing loss of refund claims
rejected_candidates.reason: Already captured by F-011 (refund key collision/overwrite/poisoning); this candidate does not add a new independent root cause.

rejected_candidates.title: onCall does not validate fromToken and toToken are legitimate ZRC20 tokens
rejected_candidates.reason: Not a distinct vulnerability in this context; payload-controlled token misuse and reserve-drain consequences are already covered by F-005/F-001/F-018.

rejected_candidates.title: Platform fees deducted before swap validation; swap failure causes permanent fee loss
rejected_candidates.reason: Incorrect: if downstream swap reverts, the entire transaction (including prior fee transfer) reverts atomically.

rejected_candidates.title: No slippage protection on output amount in onCall cross-chain receive path
rejected_candidates.reason: Unsupported: DODO swap path carries user-specified min-return parameters; this is not a distinct protocol bug as stated.

rejected_candidates.title: decodeMessage allows empty swapDataZ but onCall path lacks empty-handling in some branches
rejected_candidates.reason: Duplicate of existing F-009/F-018 coverage of empty-swap and asset-binding failures.

rejected_candidates.title: ExternalId generation uses predictable components enabling collision attacks
rejected_candidates.reason: No practical collision exploit demonstrated: hash input is coupled to caller identity and evolving nonce; front-run collision path is not substantiated.
